chapter_title.en: Chapter on Voluntary Fasting and Its Rewards on Different Days

text: 1812 - مَا قَالَهُ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لَمَّا قُتِلَ اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَمَرَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مَلَكاً فَنَادَى أَيَّتُهَا اَلْأُمَّةُ اَلظَّالِمَةُ اَلْقَاتِلَةُ عِتْرَةَ نَبِيِّهَا لاَ وَفَّقَكُمُ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى لِصَوْمٍ وَ لاَ فِطْرٍ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1812 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"When al-Husayn ibn Ali (as) was martyred, Allah (swt), the Almighty, commanded an angel to proclaim: 'O oppressive nation, O killers of the progeny of your Prophet (sw), may Allah (swt) not grant you success in fasting or breaking your fast.'"
